GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 56442 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/56442 - Title: feat: Add opt-in ACP parent completion notify for sessions_spawn - State: OPEN - Author: 1224694533jelly-boop - Labels: docs, app: web-ui, gateway, agents, size: L, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, merge-risk: 🚨 message-delivery, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 11 - Changed files: docs/tools/acp-agents.md, src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts, src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts, src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts, src/agents/acp-spawn.ts, src/agents/internal-events.ts, src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts, src/agents/subagent-announce.ts, src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts, src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts, src/gateway/protocol/schema/agent.ts Body: ```markdown ## What changed This adds an opt-in ACP parent update mode for `sessions_spawn` so ACP `mode:"run"` completions can return to the parent session like subagent completions, instead of only surfacing as parent-side system events. Key behavior changes: - adds `parentUpdates?: "system" | "notify"` for ACP spawns - keeps the default behavior unchanged - allows `parentUpdates:"notify"` for ACP `mode:"run"` even without `streamTo:"parent"` - keeps `streamTo:"parent"` as the progress relay switch - routes terminal completion/error through the existing subagent-style announce flow when `parentUpdates:"notify"` is enabled - falls back to the existing system-event path if notify/announce is unavailable ## Why Today, ACP `sessions_spawn` run completions only surface to the parent session as system events. That lets the parent session observe that work finished internally, but the completion signal primarily rides the heartbeat/internal relay path instead of a normal completion delivery path. In practice, channel-facing completion notifications can be missing or incomplete, and final result details are often lost. This change fixes that gap by adding an explicit, non-default completion routing mode for ACP run spawns. ## Design This intentionally splits progress relay from terminal completion routing: - `streamTo:"parent"` continues to control progress relay - `parentUpdates:"notify"` controls terminal completion announce That split keeps progress on the lightweight best-effort relay path while reusing the existing announce/completion machinery for final outcomes. ## Compatibility - default behavior remains unchanged - ACP / ACPX execution flow is unchanged - only parent reporting / relay / completion routing is updated ## Validation Passed: - `corepack pnpm test -- src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts` - `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 ./node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` Note: - `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` still has unrelated existing local failures: - `sessions_list filters kinds and includes messages` timeout - `sessions_send supports fire-and-forget and wait` expects `historyOnlyCalls` length 8 but gets 9 ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-03-28T14:45:33Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds an opt-in `parentUpdates: "notify"` mode for ACP `mode="run"` spawns, routing terminal completion through the existing subagent announce flow instead of the heartbeat/system-event path. The change is well-scoped, backward-compatible, and covered by targeted tests for the happy path, fallback, and error cases. Key changes: - **New `parentUpdates` parameter** on `sessions_spawn` / `spawnAcpDirect`, accepted only for `runtime="acp"` + `mode="run"` with an active requester session context. - **Progress vs. completion split**: `streamTo:"parent"` continues to control progress relay; `parentUpdates:"notify"` independently controls terminal announce routing. - **Fallback path**: if `runSubagentAnnounceFlow` is unavailable or returns `false`, the relay falls back to the existing system-event completion notice — matching the documented fallback guarantee. - **`terminalPhaseHandling` flag**: prevents any further event processing or timer callbacks once a lifecycle terminal phase is entered, correctly guarding the async announce window. - **`subagentAnnounceModulePromise` singleton**: lazily loaded once per process; test isolation is preserved because `loadFreshAcpSpawnParentStreamModulesForTest` re-imports the module. - Two minor maintenance concerns flagged (cache miss on repeated disk reads, local constant duplication) — neither blocks correctness.

Confidence Score: 5/5

The agent replays its conversation history via `session/load`. Requires `runtime: "acp"`. -- `streamTo` (optional): `"parent"` streams initial ACP run progress summaries back to the requester session as system events. +- `streamTo` (optional): `"parent"` streams initial ACP run progress summaries back to the requester session. - When available, accepted responses include `streamLogPath` pointing to a session-scoped JSONL log (`.acp-stream.jsonl`) you can tail for full relay history. +- `parentUpdates` (optional): controls how ACP `mode: "run"` spawns report back to the requester session. + - `notify` is opt-in and changes terminal completion routing so the finished ACP task is announced back into the parent session like a subagent completion. + - `notify` requires `mode: "run"` and an active requester session context. + - If the notify path is unavailable, OpenClaw falls back to the normal parent-session system-event completion notice. ### Resume an existing session diff --git a/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts b/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts --- a/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts +++ b/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.test.ts @@ -1,11 +1,17 @@ const readAcpSessionEntryMock = vi.fn(); Memory plugins that persist on `agent_end` would store an empty-content turn for these blocked/failed cases. target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #58765: Feature: Support output dimensionality truncation for local GGUF embedding models
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 58765
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/58765
- Title: Feature: Support output dimensionality truncation for local GGUF embedding models
- State: OPEN
- Author: losz5000
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

The `outputDimensionality` config field currently only works for Gemini embedding-2 models. Local GGUF embedding models (e.g., Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B) always output their full dimension (1024), with no way to truncate.

## Use Case

Many modern embedding models support Matryoshka Representation Learning (MRL), where the first N dimensions contain most of the semantic information. For example, Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B outputs 1024 dimensions but the first 256-512 dimensions are sufficient for most retrieval tasks.

Benefits of truncation:
- **50-75% reduction** in vector storage for memory search
- **Faster similarity search** as the index grows
- **Minimal accuracy loss** (<2% for MRL-trained models)

## Proposed Solution

1. Make `outputDimensionality` work for all embedding providers (not just Gemini)
2. After computing the embedding vector, simply `slice(0, N)` before storing
3. When changing the dimension, trigger a full reindex (same as Gemini behavior)

Alternative: Add a separate `truncateDimensions` field under `memorySearch.local` if keeping `outputDimensionality` Gemini-specific is intentional.

## Config Example

```json
{
  "memorySearch": {
    "provider": "local",
    "local": {
      "modelPath": "Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-Q8_0.gguf"
    },
    "outputDimensionality": 512
  }
}
```

## Bonus: Query Instruction Support

Qwen3-Embedding also supports "Instruction Aware" embeddings (prepend a task description to queries for 1-5% improvement). Adding a `queryInstruction` field would be valuable:

```json
{
  "memorySearch": {
    "queryInstruction": "Search for relevant information in intelligence notes and reports"
  }
}
```

## Environment
- OpenClaw v2026.3.24
- macOS ARM64
- Local GGUF embedding: Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B-Q8_0
```

- State: OPEN

Body:
```markdown
# Bug: google-vertex provider merged into google transport path in 2026.3.28 (regression)

## Bug Summary
In OpenClaw 2026.3.28, the `google-vertex` provider is incorrectly treated as a Google AI alias by `shouldNormalizeGoogleGenerativeAiProviderConfig`. This causes Vertex AI requests to use the wrong URL format and auth method, breaking the entire `google-vertex` provider.

## Root Cause
The changelog states:
> "Google/models: resolve Gemini 3.1 pro, flash, and flash-lite for all Google provider aliases"

The intent was to unify model ID resolution (e.g., `gemini-3.1-pro` → `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`) across all Google aliases. However, `shouldNormalizeGoogleGenerativeAiProviderConfig` included `google-vertex` in its normalization, which merged not just model ID resolution but also the **transport path and auth method** — this is an over-merge.

## Evidence This Is a Bug (Not Intentional)

1. **`google-vertex` still exists as a separate provider** — if the intent was to merge it, there would be no reason to keep the provider name. Its only purpose is different billing/auth from `google` (AI Studio).

2. **`anthropic-vertex` was NOT merged** — in the same version, `anthropic-vertex` retains independent transport, auth, and stream implementations (`anthropic-vertex-CRO19LKV.js`, `anthropic-vertex-stream.ts`). This confirms Vertex AI paths were not intentionally deprecated.

3. **SDK still supports Vertex AI** — `@google/genai` v1.47.0 fully preserves `vertexai: true`, `project`, `location`, and service account auth. No SDK-side change justifies this.

4. **Environment variables still referenced** — `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`, `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION`, `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` are still read in the code, confirming Vertex AI support was not intentionally removed.

## Impact
- URL format changed from Vertex AI style (`/projects/.../locations/.../publishers/google/models/...`) to Google AI style (`/models/...`)
- Service account auth (`google-vertex` with ADC) replaced by `GEMINI_API_KEY`, making Google Cloud billing credits unusable
- Any `google-vertex/*` model assignment silently routes to the wrong endpoint and fails

## Expected Behavior
`google-vertex` should use Vertex AI transport/auth (service account ADC, Vertex AI URL format) while still benefiting from unified model ID resolution.

## Workaround
Currently setting `models.providers.google-vertex.api = "google-generative-ai"` in config forces the correct adapter, but this loses the Vertex AI-specif

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

Body:
```markdown
# Browser Tool Enhancement Proposal

## Problem Statement

OpenClaw's `browser` tool, built on Playwright, frequently fails to interact with modern frontend frameworks (React/Vue/Svelte) due to strict hit-test and visibility checks during physical click simulation.

### Typical Failure Pattern

```
Error: Element is intercepted
  - A transparent overlay div is covering the button
  - The button uses `pointer-events: none` on a parent element
  - The element has CSS layout that offsets it visually but not in DOM coordinates
  - Animations/transitions haven't completed before the click is issued
```

This happens because `page.click()` simulates a real physical pointer interaction with full hit-testing, while modern frontend frameworks often build complex DOM structures that look interactive but fail Playwright's strict accessibility/visibility checks.

---

## Proposed Solutions

### Appendix: `javascript:` Goto Hack — Blocked

An alternative escape hatch was discovered: using Playwright's `page.goto()` with a `javascript:` URL protocol to inject JS:

```json
{
  "action": "goto",
  "url": "javascript:document.querySelector('[data-ref=\"e12\"]').click(); void(0);"
}
```

**Finding**: ClawX Gateway explicitly rejects this with:
```
GatewayClientRequestError: Navigation blocked: unsupported protocol "javascript:"
```

The Gateway has already anticipated and blocked this vector. This confirms that a proper `evaluate` action must be implemented at the routing level, not workarounded through navigation.

---

## Proposed Solutions

### Solution A: Add `force` Parameter to `click` Action (Low Cost)

Playwright natively supports `page.click(selector, { force: true })`, which skips all actionability checks (visibility, stability, animatable, receiving pointer events, etc.).

**Implementation**:

```typescript
// In browser tool's act handler
case "click":
  const force = params.force === true;
  await page.click(selector, { force });
  break;
```

**When to use**: When the element exists in the DOM but is visually "covered" by transparent layers, or when `pointer-events: none` parent elements block clicks.

---

### Solution B: Add `evaluate` Action (Escape Hatch)

Expose Playwright's `page.evaluate()` directly to allow arbitrary JavaScript execution in the page context.

**Implementation**:

```typescript
case "evaluate":
  // script: string — JavaScript code to execute in browser context
  const result = await page.evaluate(params.script);
  return { success: tru

```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #61716: [Feature]: Add model parameter prompts (context window, max_tokens, modalities) during OpenAI-compatible provider onboarding CLI flow
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 61716
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/61716
- Title: [Feature]: Add model parameter prompts (context window, max_tokens, modalities) during OpenAI-compatible provider onboarding CLI flow
- State: OPEN
- Author: Andy-Xie-1145
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

Add interactive model parameter prompts (context window, max output tokens, modalities) during OpenAI-compatible provider onboarding.

### Problem to solve

When running `openclaw onboard` and selecting an OpenAI-compatible provider, users can pick a model but cannot configure model-specific parameters. Setting context window, max output tokens, and supported modalities requires manually editing `~/.openclaw/config.yaml`, which is error-prone, discouraged for non-technical users, and easy to break YAML formatting.

### Proposed solution

After the model selection step in the `openclaw onboard` flow for OpenAI-compatible providers, add interactive prompts for the following optional parameters:

- **Context Window** (e.g. `128000`) — maximum input context length
- **Max Output Tokens** (e.g. `4096`) — maximum tokens the model can generate
- **Modalities** (e.g. `text`, `text+image`, `audio`, `video`) — supported input/output modes

All prompts should be optional (allow skipping with sensible defaults).

### Alternatives considered

- **Manual config editing**: Works but is error-prone and not user-friendly.
- **Separate CLI command** (e.g. `openclaw models configure`): Adds an extra step instead of collecting all info during onboarding.

### Impact

- **Affected**: All users configuring OpenAI-compatible providers via CLI onboarding
- **Severity**: Medium — not a blocker, but forces users into unsafe config editing
- **Frequency**: Every time a new OpenAI-compatible model is added
- **Consequence**: Misconfigured context window causes truncated prompts; missing modality config prevents image/audio input from being sent correctly

### Evidence/examples

Current workaround requires manual editing:
```yaml
providers:
  openai-compatible:
    models:
      my-model:
        contextWindow: 128000
        maxOutputTokens: 4096
        modalities: [text, image]
```

### Additional information

Related: bundled providers (Qwen, Fireworks, StepFun, etc.) likely handle this internally. OpenAI-compatible custom providers should have parity.
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #62109: Interactive runs fail with auth-style 403 when custom OpenAI-compatible provider baseUrl uses Unicode/IDN or punycode hostname, but ASCII hostname/IP works
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 62109
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/62109
- Title: Interactive runs fail with auth-style 403 when custom OpenAI-compatible provider baseUrl uses Unicode/IDN or punycode hostname, but ASCII hostname/IP works
- State: OPEN
- Author: nboody
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-info, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦐 gold shrimp

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

OpenClaw interactive runs (Web UI and local TUI / embedded path) fail with:

- `403 Your request was blocked.`
- `failoverReason: "auth"`
- `profileFailureReason: "auth"`

when a custom OpenAI-compatible provider uses either:

- a Unicode/IDN hostname in `baseUrl`
- or the punycode form of that same hostname

The same backend works when accessed via:

- ASCII hostname
- public IP + dedicated `/v1`-only proxy port

This suggests an issue in OpenClaw's handling of non-ASCII-origin hostnames (including punycode forms) in the interactive/embedded run path.

---

### Environment

- OpenClaw version: `2026.4.5`
- Host: Linux
- Surface:
  - Web UI
  - local TUI
- Provider type:
  - custom OpenAI-compatible provider
  - `api: openai-completions`

---

### Affected behavior

With a provider config like this:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "custom_provider": {
        "baseUrl": "https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1",
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "apiKey": "<redacted>",
        "models": [
          {
            "id": "gpt-5.4",
            "name": "gpt-5.4",
            "contextWindow": 200000,
            "maxTokens": 8192
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "model": {
        "primary": "custom_provider/gpt-5.4"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

interactive runs fail before a usable assistant reply is produced.

Relevant logs:

```text
error: 403 Your request was blocked.
failoverReason: auth
profileFailureReason: auth
provider: custom_provider
model: gpt-5.4
```

---

### Key observation

The backend itself is healthy.

Direct manual requests to the same backend succeed:

```bash
curl https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
  --data '{"model":"gpt-5.4","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"reply with exactly: ok"}],"max_tokens":8}'
```

This returns HTTP 200 and `ok`.

The same is true for the punycode hostname and for an ASCII hostname.

So the failure is not simply "backend unavailable".

---

### What was tested

#### 1. Different backends
Two different OpenAI-compatible backends were tested:

- a LiteLLM-based backend
- a different OAuth-backed OpenAI-compatible backend

Manual requests succeeded against both backends.

#### 2. Unicode hostname
Using a Unicode/IDN hostname in `baseUrl` caused interactive runs to fail with auth-style 403.

Example shape:

- `https://<unicode-idn-domain>/v1`


[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 62134 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/62134 - Title: feat: OAuth authentication for remote MCP servers - State: OPEN - Author: nrcrews - Labels: agents, size: XL, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 10 - Changed files: src/agents/mcp-http.ts, src/agents/mcp-oauth-provider.test.ts, src/agents/mcp-oauth-provider.ts, src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts, src/agents/mcp-transport-config.ts, src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts, src/agents/mcp-transport.ts, src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.oauth.test.ts, src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts, src/config/types.mcp.ts Body: ```markdown Summary - Problem: OAuth-protected remote MCP servers are unusable — the only auth mechanism is static headers, which break when tokens are short-lived (minutes) - Why it matters: The MCP Authorization spec defines OAuth as the standard auth flow. Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP clients support it. OpenClaw does not, blocking users from connecting to a growing number of OAuth-protected MCP services. - What changed: Pass the SDK's existing authProvider option to StreamableHTTPClientTransport and SSEClientTransport when auth: "oauth" is configured. New OAuthClientProvider adapter handles browser redirect, local callback server, and token persistence. UnauthorizedError caught during connection triggers the flow automatically. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to existing server configurations. No new dependencies. No changes to stdio transport. No CLI commands added (follow-up). No device flow / headless auth (would require MCP spec extension). Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-06T21:28:54Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds OAuth 2.0 authentication support for remote MCP servers via a new `OAuthClientProvider` adapter, local HTTP callback server on port 8093, and JSON token persistence under `~/.openclaw/mcp-auth/`. The implementation threads the new provider through transport resolution and adds a retry path on `UnauthorizedError`. - **P1 – callback server starts after the browser redirect is already triggered**: In `pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`, the initial `connectWithTimeout` call causes the SDK to invoke `redirectToAuthorization` (opening the browser) *before* throwing `UnauthorizedError`. `waitForOAuthCallback` — which starts the HTTP listener — is only called inside the `catch` block. An SSO session that auto-redirects without user interaction can complete the browser redirect before port 8093 is listening, silently dropping the authorization code and leaving the flow stuck until the 2-minute timeout. - **P2 – `toSafeFilename` collision**: Two distinct server names like `"my-server"` and `"my server"` both map to `my_server.json`, so one server's tokens will silently overwrite another's. - **P2 – no CSRF `state` parameter**: The `OAuthClientProvider` doesn't implement `state()`, so no state token is generated or appended to the authorization URL. `waitForOAuthCallback` also drops the `state` query parameter from the callback. Any local process can hit the callback endpoint with an arbitrary code during the 120- [comments/context truncated after 1500 chars] ``` Diff/context: ```diff Subject: [PATCH 1/5] feat: OAuth authentication for remote MCP servers Wire up the MCP SDK's existing authProvider support on StreamableHTTPClientTransport and SSEClientTransport so OpenClaw can authenticate to OAuth-protected remote MCP servers. - Problem: OAuth-protected remote MCP servers are unusable — the only auth mechanism is static headers, which break when tokens are short-lived (minutes). - Why it matters: The MCP Authorization spec defines OAuth as the standard auth flow. Claude Code, Cursor, and other MCP clients support it. OpenClaw does not, blocking users from connecting to a growing number of OAuth-protected MCP services. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to existing server configurations. The change threads the auth provider through MCP transport resolution and adds retry on UnauthorizedError. Core files: mcp-oauth-provider.ts, mcp-transport.ts, pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts. Config adds auth: 'oauth' field on McpServerConfig.

Missing CSRF state parameter noted by Greptile.
Token persistence is plain JSON (consistent with existing pattern but unencrypted).

Logging could ignore a valid configured `logging.file` path because the logging runtime depended on a fragile built-module require path. 2. Local memory embeddings could fail hard on VRAM-fit initialization instead of retrying and degrading gracefully. ## Changes - switch logging config reads to the real source config loader - expand `YYYY-MM-DD` placeholders in configured log file paths before writing - add GPU-fit local embedding init with one retry on VRAM-style failure - fall back to CPU-only for that embedding operation if the second GPU-fit attempt also fails - add targeted tests for both behaviors ## Reproduction ### Logging Configure: `logging.file = /home/user/.openclaw/logs/openclaw-YYYY-MM-DD.log` Before: - runtime could miss the configured path and fall back elsewhere - placeholder expansion was not honored at write time After: - the configured path is resolved through the normal config loader - `YYYY-MM-DD` expands to the real local date before file writes ### Local embeddings Use local memory embeddings with `node-llama-cpp` on a machine where GPU-fit init can fail with: `A context size of 24 is too large for the available VRAM` Before: - the operation failed unless GPU was disabled globally After: - retry once with a fresh GPU-fit init - if it still fails, fall back to CPU-only for that operation - the next operation starts fresh on the normal GPU-fit path ## Validation Ran: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/logging/config.test.ts src/logging/logger-settings.test.ts src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.test.ts` Result: - 3 test files passed - 28 tests passed ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-07T22:24:38Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR fixes two independent local-setup issues: logging config is now read via the real `loadConfig()` import (dropping the fragile `resolveNodeRequireFromMeta` indirection) and `YYYY-MM-DD` placeholders are expanded at write time; local embedding initialization now retries once with a fresh GPU-fit calculation on VRAM errors and falls back to a temporary CPU-only context if the retry also fails. - The temporary CPU-mode `Llama` instance created in `createTemporaryCpuContext` is never disposed — `cpuContext` and `cpuModel` are cleaned up, but `cpuLlama` is dropped, leaking CPU thread-pool resources on every fallback operation.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge after fixing the cpuLlama disposal leak in the CPU fallback path. The logging fixes are clean and well-tested. The embedding retry/fallback logic is correct in the sequential case and the tests are solid, but the missing cpuLlama.dispose() call is a genuine resource leak that will accumulate on any machine that repeatedly triggers the CPU fallback. The concurrent VRAM race is a real but lower-probability concern. Score of 4 reflects the single clear P1 that should be fixed before landing. src/memory-host-sdk/host/embeddings.ts — createTemporaryCpuContext dispose function is missing cpuLlama.dispose()

Vulnerabilities

 No security concerns identified. Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Implements macOS `sandbox-exec` wrapping for the `exec()` tool, addressing Issue #58730.

## Changes

- **`src/agents/sandbox-exec-wrapper.ts`** — Built-in Seatbelt profile definitions for macOS sandbox
- **`src/agents/bash-tools.sandbox-exec.test.ts`** — Unit tests for sandbox profile generation
- **`src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`** — Added `sandboxProfile` option to `runExecProcess()`
- **`src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`** — Added `sandboxProfile` parameter to exec tool schema

## New Option

```
exec({ command: "rm -rf /", sandboxProfile: "default" })
```

| Profile | Network | File Read | File Write |
|---------|---------|-----------|------------|
| `default` | Denied | /tmp, ~/openclaw-workspace, /usr, /System | /tmp, ~/openclaw-workspace |
| `permissive` | Allowed | All | All |

## Caveat

`sandbox-exec` is deprecated on macOS (App Sandbox recommended for production).

Closes #58730

```

GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 63145
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63145
- Title: [Feature]: openclaw models status --probe --all — per-model health check across all configured models
- State: OPEN
- Author: ashok-2003
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
# [Feature]: openclaw models status --probe --all — per-model health check across all configured models

### Summary

Add a --all flag to models status --probe that sends a real completion request to every configured model (primary + fallbacks) and reports per-model health, not just per-provider auth.

### Problem to solve

`models status --probe` currently validates **provider authentication**, not individual **model IDs**. This means:

1. **Deprecated/removed models go undetected** — A model like `openai/gpt-5.4` can sit in config returning 400s, and `--probe` will still report the provider as healthy because the API key is valid.

2. **Fallback chain health is invisible** — If your primary model fails and OpenClaw falls back to a model that's deprecated or rate-limited, the entire chain breaks silently. There's no way to pre-validate the fallback sequence.

3. **Multi-model configs are increasingly common** — Users on OpenRouter routinely configure 10-15+ models across tiers (primary, fallbacks, image models). The current probe tests one model per provider, leaving the rest unchecked.

The only workaround today is manually curling each model endpoint or writing a bash loop — which requires knowing each provider's API format and isn't integrated with OpenClaw's config resolution.

### Proposed solution

Add `--all` flag to `openclaw models status --probe`:

```bash
openclaw models status --probe --all
```

**Behavior:**
- Iterate over every model in `agents.defaults.model.primary`, `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`, and `agents.defaults.imageModel` (plus per-agent overrides if `--agent` is specified)
- Send a minimal completion request (e.g., `max_tokens: 1`, prompt: "hi") to each model ID through the configured provider
- Report per-model status:

✅ anthropic/claude-opus-4-6        ok         (1.2s)
✅ openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v3  ok         (0.8s)
⚠️ openrouter/google/gemini-3.1-pro rate_limited (429, retry: 30s)
❌ openrouter/openai/gpt-5.4        error      (400: model not found)
❌ openrouter/moonshot/kimi-k2.5    timeout    (10.0s)

**JSON output** (`--json`):
Each entry should include: `model`, `provider`, `status` (ok | rate_limited | error | timeout | no_auth), `latencyMs`, `httpStatus`, `message`.

**Scope options:**
- `--probe --all` — every configured model
- `--probe --all --provider openrouter` — only models under a specific provider
- `--probe` (current behavior unchanged) — primary model / auth check only

**Timeout:** Respect existing p

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #63760: Announce runs for subagent/ACP completions waste tokens on cold cache with parent model
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 63760
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/63760
- Title: Announce runs for subagent/ACP completions waste tokens on cold cache with parent model
- State: OPEN
- Author: liu51115
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

When a subagent or ACP session (e.g. Claude Code) completes, OpenClaw runs an "announce" embedded run to deliver the result to the parent agent. This announce run:

1. **Uses the parent agent's model** — e.g. Opus at $25/MTok output for what is essentially a message relay
2. **Starts with a cold cache** — loads the full agent context (system prompt, conversation history, pinned files) fresh, paying full cache-write costs
3. **Does minimal work** — rewrites "CC finished, here is the result" in the agent's voice

### Cost impact

For an Opus agent with ~80k context:
- Cache write: ~80k × $6.25/MTok ≈ $0.50
- Output: ~200 tokens × $25/MTok ≈ $0.005
- **Total: ~$0.50+ per announcement**, for a message that could be delivered for ~$0.03 on Sonnet

With frequent CC spawns (10-20/day), this adds up to $5-10/day in announce overhead alone.

## Proposed solutions

1. **`announce.model` config** — allow overriding the model used for announce embedded runs (e.g. use Sonnet instead of parent model)
2. **`announce.lightContext`** — strip conversation history from announce runs, only include the completion result + minimal system prompt
3. **Both** — light context + cheaper model would reduce announce cost by ~95%

## Current workaround

Setting `delivery: { mode: "none" }` on spawns and manually checking report files. Works but loses the automatic notification flow.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.9
- Parent agent: Opus 4.6 (Anthropic direct)
- ACP backend: Claude Code
```

Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation. - Problem: ACP sessions could report a successful `/acp spawn` even when the newly initialized runtime handle was already unusable, and stale gateway disconnects could leave cancel/close/early-turn recovery stuck on a bad cached handle. - Why it matters: Operators can see contradictory UX (`Spawned` followed by immediate `ACP_TURN_FAILED`) and then lose reliable recovery controls for the affected ACP session. - What changed: Probe the freshly initialized runtime status before reporting spawn success; clean up failed post-spawn sessions; treat explicit stale gateway disconnect close codes as recoverable for close, cancel, active-turn cancel, and early-turn retry. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No ACP protocol/schema changes, no new public commands, no auth/provider changes, no gateway restart policy changes, and no broad retry policy for non-stale gateway errors. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # (none) - Related #63656 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. Test coverage is added for all four new code paths: post-spawn health failure, close recovery, cancel recovery (passive path), and early-turn retry.

No files require special attention.

 No security concerns identified. github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 63826 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63826 - Title: security: fix HIGH/CRITICAL vulns in skill scanner, SSRF, hook priority, and token verification - State: OPEN - Author: hawonb711-tech - Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 security-boundary, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 3 - Changed files: src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts, src/plugins/registry.ts, src/security/skill-scanner.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary Security audit identified 4 HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities. This PR fixes all of them with minimal, targeted changes. ### 1. Skill Scanner Bypass (CRITICAL) — `src/security/skill-scanner.ts` All 9 existing scanner rules are bypassable via basic JavaScript obfuscation (dynamic property access, import aliasing, prototype pollution, indirect eval). **Fix:** Add 6 new LINE_RULES and 3 new SOURCE_RULES: - `dangerous-exec-dynamic`: detects `cp["exec"]()` bracket notation - `dangerous-exec-indirect`: detects `Reflect.apply`, `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` - `dynamic-code-execution-indirect`: detects `globalThis[...]`, `.constructor.constructor` - `prototype-pollution`: detects `__proto__`, `Object.prototype.x =` - `import-aliasing`: detects `{ exec: aliasName }` destructuring - Unicode escape sequences, `String.fromCharCode`, dynamic `import()` of dangerous modules - Lower base64 threshold from 200→100 chars, hex threshold from 6→4 sequences - Expand network API coverage: `http.get`, `XMLHttpRequest`, `WebSocket`, `axios` ### 2. SSRF via Unguarded fetch() (HIGH) — `src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts` `discoverOpenAICompatibleLocalModels()` uses raw `fetch()` with user-provided `baseUrl`, enabling SSRF to cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254), internal services, and API key leakage. **Fix:** Replace `fetch()` with `fetchWithSsrFGuard()` using `allowPrivateNetwork: true` (needed for legitimate local providers) and proper dispatcher release. ### 3. Hook Priority TOCTOU (CRITICAL) — `src/plugins/registry.ts` No upper bound on hook `priority` allows malicious plugins to register at priority 9999, running before security-critical hooks to auto-approve dangerous tool calls, inject prompts, or tamper transcripts. **Fix:** Cap priority for external plugins at 100 and bundled plugins at 1000. Log diagnostic warning when priority is clamped. ### 4. Device Token Revocation TOCTOU (HIGH) — `src/infra/device-pairing.ts` `verifyDeviceToken()` checks `revokedAtMs` early but mutates state later. A concurrent `revokeDeviceToken()` can revoke the token between check and state mutation. **Fix:** Re-check `revokedAtMs` immediately before `lastUsedAtMs` update to narrow the TOCTOU window. ## PoC Documents Detailed proof-of-concept documents with exploit code are included in `security-pocs/`: - `01-skill-scanner-bypass.md` — 5 complete bypass techniques - `02-ssrf-unguarded-fetch.md` — 4 SSRF attack scenarios - `03-hook-priority-toctou.md` — 4 hook [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-09T16:07:55Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR addresses four reported security issues: skill-scanner bypass, SSRF via unguarded `fetch()`, hook priority manipulation, and a device-token revocation race. The scanner and hook-priority changes are solid; the SSRF and TOCTOU fixes each have a correctness problem that needs attention before merge. - **P1 — dispatcher leak** (`src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts`): `release` is scoped inside the `try` block; the `catch` handler cannot call it, so the undici dispatcher leaks if `response.json()` throws. A `finally` block (or hoisting `release` before the `try`) is needed. - **P1 — TOCTOU fix is a no-op** (`src/infra/device-pairing.ts`): both `revokedAtMs` checks sit inside the same `withLock()` closure — the mutex already prevents any concurrent mutation between them. The actual race is external (caller acts on `{ ok: true }` after the lock releases); the patch does not address it.

Confidence Score: 3/5

Two P1 defects — a dispatcher resource leak and a no-op TOCTOU mitigation — should be resolved before merging. The hook-priority cap and scanner rule additions are correct and well-constructed. However, the SSRF fix has a real dispatcher leak in the exception path, and the device-pairing TOCTOU patch doesn't actually reduce the race window it claims to fix. Both are present defects on the changed path. src/plugins/provider-self-hosted-setup.ts (release() leak), src/infra/device-pairin [comments/context truncated after 1500 chars] ``` Diff/context: ds4_description: PR fixing 4 HIGH/CRITICAL security vulnerabilities: skill scanner bypass (adds 6 new LINE_RULES and 3 SOURCE_RULES to detect obfuscated dangerous patterns), SSRF via unguarded fetch in provider-self-hosted-setup (replaces raw fetch with SSRF-guarded fetch), hook priority TOCTOU in registry.ts (caps external plugin priority at 100, bundled at 1000), and device token revocation race in device-pairing.ts (re-checks revokedAtMs before lastUsedAtMs update). Greptile notes a dispatcher leak in the SSRF fix and that the TOCTOU fix is a no-op under the existing mutex.
ds4_caveats: Greptile identified two P1 defects: SSRF fix has a dispatcher leak (release() scoped inside try, unreachable in catch), and device token TOCTOU fix is a no-op (both checks are inside the same mutex closure). Skill scanner rules and hook priority cap are considered correct.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #64081: Feature: config-level default task notify policy
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 64081
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64081
- Title: Feature: config-level default task notify policy
- State: OPEN
- Author: ancxlol
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:message-loss, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Problem

Background task completion notifications (subagent and ACP) default to `done_only` policy, which sends a delivery message to the requester channel (e.g. Telegram) for every task completion. 

When running multiple sub-agents or ACP sessions, this creates notification spam with messages like:
```
Background task done: my-task-label (run 11054).
Background task failed: my-task-label (run 11037). Permission prompt unavailable in non-interactive mode
```

These messages are in a fixed format that is not user-friendly or natural language. The agent (main session) already receives the completion event internally and can relay results to the user in natural language — making the raw task notification redundant and noisy.

## Current behavior

- `done_only` is hardcoded as default for subagent/ACP tasks in `task-registry-*.js`
- Cron tasks correctly default to `silent`
- The only way to change policy is per-task via `openclaw tasks notify <id> silent`, which is racy (notification may fire before the policy change takes effect)
- There is no config key to set a default notify policy

## Proposed solution

Add a configuration key to allow users to set the default task notify policy, for example:

```json5
{
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      tasks: {
        defaultNotifyPolicy: "silent"  // or "done_only" | "state_changes"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Or at a more granular level per runtime type:

```json5
{
  agents: {
    defaults: {
      tasks: {
        notifyPolicy: {
          subagent: "silent",
          acp: "silent",
          cron: "silent",   // already silent
          cli: "silent"     // already silent
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

This would allow users who prefer the agent to handle user-facing updates to suppress raw task notifications, while keeping the default `done_only` behavior for users who want it.

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.4.8
- Channel: Telegram (direct chat)
- Use case: heavy sub-agent/ACP orchestration with agent-mediated user updates
```

- Maintainers can also comment `@clawsweeper review` to request a fresh review only.

- Maintainers can comment `@clawsweeper explain` to ask for more context, or `@clawsweeper stop` to stop active automation.

</details>

This PR fixes a real ACP session collision bug: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` was returning the parent channel ID as `conversationId` on a parent-match, causing all threads under the same parent to materialise into the same session key. The fix correctly separates concerns — `matchInboundConversation` now returns only `matchPriority`, and session key materialisation always uses the live `ConversationRef` — and is backed by a regression test. The `sendFarewell` suppression in `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` is correctly scoped to `expectsCompletionMessage` runs to prevent duplicate delivery.

Safe to merge; all remaining findings are P2 style suggestions with no runtime impact. target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #6599: Feature: Add /models test-fallback command to verify fallback chain
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 6599
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/6599
- Title: Feature: Add /models test-fallback command to verify fallback chain
- State: OPEN
- Author: jonwiley
- Labels: enhancement, P3, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🌊 off-meta tidepool

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Add a command to test that the model fallback chain is correctly configured and working, without waiting for a real failure.

## Motivation

Currently, there's no way to verify fallback configuration works until an actual provider failure occurs. This makes it hard to:

1. Confirm fallback models are correctly configured and accessible
2. Verify API keys/auth profiles for fallback providers are valid
3. Debug fallback issues proactively rather than discovering them during outages

## Proposed Solution

A command like `/models test-fallback` that:

1. Intentionally skips the primary model
2. Attempts a simple request to the first fallback model
3. Reports success/failure and which model responded

Could also support `/models test-fallback --all` to test the entire chain.

## Alternatives Considered

- Manually breaking the primary auth to force fallback (disruptive)
- Spawning a subagent with an invalid primary model (doesn't test the actual fallback mechanism)
- Trusting the config and waiting for natural failures (reactive, not proactive)

## Additional Context

Related PRs working on fallback improvements:
- #6381 - trigger fallback chain on unknown model errors
- #4217 - validate model names in fallbacks commands
- #3253 - per-model fallbacks support

---
*Filed via openclaw AI assistant after reviewing CONTRIBUTING.md*
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #6615: Feature: Add denylist support for exec-approvals
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 6615
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/6615
- Title: Feature: Add denylist support for exec-approvals
- State: OPEN
- Author: aaroneden
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:data-loss, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Add **denylist** support to exec-approvals, complementing the existing allowlist. This enables "allow everything except X" policies.

## Use Case

I want to:
- Allow all commands to run without prompts
- **Except** block/prompt for specific dangerous commands like:
  - `gog gmail send` (sending emails)
  - `gog gmail delete` (deleting emails)  
  - `beeper-send.sh` (sending messages)

Currently, exec-approvals only supports:
- `security: "deny"` - block all
- `security: "allowlist"` - allow only listed commands
- `security: "full"` - allow everything

There's no way to say "allow everything except these specific patterns."

## Proposed Solution

Add a `denylist` array to exec-approvals config:

```json
{
  "defaults": {
    "security": "denylist",
    "ask": "on-match",
    "denylist": [
      {
        "id": "DENY-EMAIL-SEND",
        "pattern": "/opt/homebrew/bin/gog",
        "argsMatch": "gmail send",
        "reason": "Sending emails requires approval"
      },
      {
        "id": "DENY-EMAIL-DELETE",
        "pattern": "/opt/homebrew/bin/gog", 
        "argsMatch": "gmail delete",
        "reason": "Use 'gmail archive' instead"
      },
      {
        "id": "DENY-BEEPER-SEND",
        "pattern": "*/beeper-send.sh",
        "reason": "Sending messages requires approval"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Key additions:
- `security: "denylist"` mode - allow everything except matches
- `argsMatch` field - match against command arguments, not just binary path
- `ask: "on-match"` - prompt when denylist matches (vs blocking outright)

## Current Workarounds

1. Use `security: "full"` and hope agent guardrails work (unreliable)
2. Use `security: "allowlist"` with a massive list of safe commands (tedious)
3. Create wrapper scripts (fragile)

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.1.30
- macOS
```

This is a common pain point. The allow-everything-except pattern is what most developers actually want, because maintaining an exhaustive allowlist is impractical and a full deny breaks the workflow.

Your proposed denylist approach is solid. A few thoughts from building a similar system:

**Pattern matching on arguments, not just binary paths, is the right call.** The same binary (`gog`) can be safe or dangerous depending on arguments. Matching on the full command string with regex gives you the granularity you need without maintaining separate entries for every subcommand.

**The tri-state model (allow / deny / require-approval) covers more ground than just deny.** Your `ask: "on-match"` is essentially "require-approval," which is the right default for most denylist matches. Hard-deny should be reserved for things that are never acceptable (like `rm -rf /` or posting credentials). Everything else benefits from a human confirmation step rather th

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66252: [Feature]: Per-Agent TTS/STT Configuration Overrides for Multi-Language Support
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 66252
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66252
- Title: [Feature]: Per-Agent TTS/STT Configuration Overrides for Multi-Language Support
- State: OPEN
- Author: acwilan
- Labels: enhancement, P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:auth-provider, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Summary

Add support for agent-specific text-to-speech (TTS) and speech-to-text (STT) configuration overrides, enabling multiple agents in a single OpenClaw instance to use different voices, languages, or providers.

### Problem to solve

Currently, TTS/STT configuration is global under messages.tts and tools.media.audio. All agents share the same voice, language, and provider settings. This prevents:

• Multi-language agents: Cannot have one agent speak English and another Spanish
• Voice differentiation: Cannot give different agents distinct voices/personalities
• Provider optimization: Cannot use different TTS providers for different use cases (e.g., fast vs. quality)

### Proposed solution

Extend the agent configuration schema to support TTS/STT overrides:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": {
      "spanish-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "Eres un asistente útil...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "macvoice",
          "voice": "Monica",  // Spanish voice
          "rate": 0.5
        },
        "stt": {
          "provider": "deepgram",
          "language": "es"
        }
      },
      "english-assistant": {
        "systemPrompt": "You are a helpful assistant...",
        "tts": {
          "provider": "elevenlabs",
          "voice": "Adam"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Implementation notes:

• Overrides merge with global messages.tts config (agent-specific wins)
• Passed to speech providers via SpeechSynthesisRequest.providerOverrides or new agentConfig field
• Optional: Support `` directive tags as runtime override

### Alternatives considered

1. Separate OpenClaw instances: High resource overhead, complex management
2. Model-driven language detection: Unreliable, adds latency, doesn't solve voice differentiation
3. Global config switching: Requires restart, not dynamic per conversation

### Impact

• Users: Enable multi-language households, personalized agents, voice branding
• Plugins: Providers receive richer context; no breaking changes
• Ecosystem: Differentiation feature for OpenClaw vs. competitors

### Evidence/examples

• Discord communities: Spanish + English channels need different agent personalities
• Customer service: Escalation bot (professional voice) vs. casual helper (friendly voice)
• Accessibility: Different voices help users distinguish agent roles

### Additional information

• Related: macvoice plugin supports voice selection; lacks agent context
• Similar features: Agent-specific model already e

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #66467: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 66467
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66467
- Title: ACP session/update usage_update notification fails validation when used=null (Invalid params / expected object, received undefined)
- State: OPEN
- Author: fisher1006
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Discord-bound ACP sessions can fail during `session/update` notification handling when the ACP runtime emits a `usage_update` notification with `used: null`.

In the observed failure, OpenClaw logs:

```text
Error handling notification {
  jsonrpc: '2.0',
  method: 'session/update',
  params: {
    sessionId: 'ba706432-d8f3-431b-ad57-bd0f0a5dc131',
    update: {
      sessionUpdate: 'usage_update',
      used: null,
      size: 200000,
      cost: [Object]
    }
  }
} {
  code: -32602,
  message: 'Invalid params'
}
```

And the validation payload includes repeated schema errors such as:

```text
Invalid input: expected object, received undefined
sessionUpdate: { _errors: [Array] }
title: { _errors: [Array] }
toolCallId: { _errors: [Array] }
entries: { _errors: [Array] }
availableCommands: { _errors: [Array] }
currentModeId: { _errors: [Array] }
configOptions: { _errors: [Array] }
used: { _errors: [Array] }
```

This appears to be a runtime/schema mismatch in OpenClaw's ACP notification handling, not a generic "session missing" issue.

## Why this seems distinct from existing ACP/Discord issues

I checked existing issues around:

- Discord-bound ACP persistent resume failures / binding restore
- generic `ACP_TURN_FAILED`
- Discord slash/interaction `40060 Interaction has already been acknowledged`

Those are related symptoms in the same area, but this repro is narrower:

- the ACP session **does exist**
- the runtime **does emit** `session/update`
- the failure happens when OpenClaw handles a specific `usage_update` payload
- the bad payload shape includes `used: null`

So this looks like a notification schema/validator bug, not just a Discord binding problem.

## Environment

- OpenClaw: local main install on macOS arm64
- OS: macOS Darwin 25.x (Apple Silicon)
- Channel: Discord
- ACP backend: bundled `acpx`
- Runtime context: Discord-bound ACP thread/session path

## Evidence

### 1) ACP sessions are being created

`openclaw status` showed Discord ACP binding sessions being created successfully, so this is not a total runtime outage and not simply "session was never created".

### 2) Runtime code suggests `usage_update` should be accepted

In bundled ACPX runtime code:

`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/acpx/node_modules/acpx/dist/runtime.js`

there is explicit handling for `usage_update`:

```js
case "usage_update": {
  const used = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.used);
  const size = asOptionalFiniteNumber(payload.size

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #68497: [Feature]: Expose canvas A2UI action bridge to web browsers (WebSocket fallback for window.openclawSendUserAction)
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 68497
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/68497
- Title: [Feature]: Expose canvas A2UI action bridge to web browsers (WebSocket fallback for window.openclawSendUserAction)
- State: OPEN
- Author: n3wth
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr, clawsweeper:needs-maintainer-review, clawsweeper:needs-product-decision, clawsweeper:needs-security-review, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:security, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
## Context

The canvas host at `/__openclaw__/canvas/` serves a test page (`index.html` inside the `canvas-host` dist and the default `~/.openclaw/canvas/index.html`) with Hello / Time / Photo / Dalek buttons. The page status shows:

```
Bridge: missing · iOS=no · Android=no
Failed to send action: dalek
```

when opened in any desktop web browser.

## Root cause

The test page checks for three bridges:

```js
const hasIOS = () => !!(window.webkit?.messageHandlers?.openclawCanvasA2UIAction)
const hasAndroid = () => !!(window.openclawCanvasA2UIAction?.postMessage)
const hasHelper = () => typeof window.openclawSendUserAction === "function"
```

None are injected in a regular browser context, so every button falls through with `Failed to send action: <name>`. By design, the A2UI action path only works when the canvas is loaded inside an iOS/Android native OpenClaw node.

## Request

Expose `window.openclawSendUserAction` in the **web** canvas bundle too, with the action routed back to the Gateway via the existing WebSocket (or a dedicated HTTP POST `/__openclaw__/canvas/action`). This would let the canvas be useful from:

- `canvas.newth.io` style reverse-proxied deployments (behind Cloudflare Access / equivalent)
- Desktop dev loops where the author doesn't want to round-trip through an iOS/Android node just to test a button
- Embedded views (iframe from Control UI, dashboards, etc.)

## Suggested shape

```js
// Injected by canvas-host when running without a native bridge
window.openclawSendUserAction = ({ name, surfaceId, sourceComponentId, context }) => {
  const payload = { name, surfaceId, sourceComponentId, context, source: 'web' }
  // POST to same-origin endpoint; Gateway auth already established by browser
  fetch('/__openclaw__/canvas/action', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    credentials: 'same-origin',
  }).then(r => r.ok ? dispatchStatus({ ok: true, id: payload.name })
                    : dispatchStatus({ ok: false, error: `HTTP ${r.status}` }))
  return true
}
```

Auth: the Gateway already guards `/__openclaw__/` with the gateway token (verified: hitting the canvas URL without the `Authorization: Bearer` header returns 401). So the endpoint inherits auth for free.

## Version

- OpenClaw `v2026.4.15`
- macOS Sonoma (arm64), served via `openclaw gateway` launchd agent
- Reproduced in Chrome 147 and Safari 18
```

github_context:
GitHub item:

- Title: feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models
- State: OPEN
- Author: wirjo
- Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof

Body:
```markdown
# feat(amazon-bedrock-mantle): add known context windows for open-weight Mantle models

## Problem

Mantle's `/v1/models` endpoint returns only model IDs — no token limit metadata. Discovery hardcodes `contextWindow: 32000` for every model, which is wrong for most:

- MiniMax M2/M2.1: **1,000,000** (gets 32K)
- Qwen3 Coder: **256,000** (gets 32K)
- DeepSeek V3.x, GLM 4.x, Nemotron: **128,000** (gets 32K)

This causes the same premature context overflow and wrong compaction thresholds as #65952 (the amazon-bedrock equivalent).

## Fix

Add a `KNOWN_CONTEXT_WINDOWS` lookup table for open-weight models currently available on Mantle. Raise the default fallback from 32K → 128K for unknown models.

```
MiniMax M2 / M2.1              → 1,000,000
Qwen3 Coder (480B/Next/30B)    → 256,000
Qwen3 235B / 32B               → 128,000
DeepSeek V3.1 / V3.2           → 128,000
GLM 4.6 / 4.7 / 4.7 Flash      → 128,000
NVIDIA Nemotron (all variants)  → 128,000
```

**Claude models are intentionally excluded** — they use a separate Anthropic-native endpoint on Mantle (`/anthropic/v1/messages`), not the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint that this extension handles. Claude model metadata is managed by the `amazon-bedrock` extension (#65952).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts` | +41/-2 — context window map, `resolveKnownContextWindow()`, default bump |

Single file, no type system changes.

## References

- [Mantle model list](https://conoroneill.net/2026/02/20/aws-bedrock-mantle-model-names-youve-been-looking-for/) (community, Feb 2026)
- [Opus 4.7 model card](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/model-card-anthropic-claude-opus-4-7.html) — confirms Claude uses `/anthropic/v1/messages` on Mantle, not Chat Completions
- Related: #65952 (same pattern for amazon-bedrock discovery)

Labels: size: S, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, status: 📣 needs proof
```

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #68916: [Bug]: ACP oneshot sessions leave orphaned processes — session reset does not clean up child ACP session keys
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 68916
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/68916
- Title: [Bug]: ACP oneshot sessions leave orphaned processes — session reset does not clean up child ACP session keys
- State: OPEN
- Author: shadow-enthusiast
- Labels: P1, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:source-repro, impact:session-state, impact:crash-loop, issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When `sessions_spawn` creates ACP sessions with `mode: "run"`, the spawned `claude-agent-acp` + `claude` processes remain alive indefinitely after the run completes. Additionally, when the **parent** session is reset via `/new` or `/reset`, the child ACP sessions are not cleaned up — the gateway only calls `closeAcpRuntimeForSession()` for the parent session key, not for child session keys spawned from it.

This results in progressive memory exhaustion. On a 16 GB machine, 11 orphaned pairs accumulated ~3.5 GB within 2 hours of normal use.

### Related issues

This is closely related to #52708, #61895, and #35886 but identifies a **specific additional gap**: the parent session reset path does not enumerate and close child ACP sessions.

### Steps to reproduce

1. From a main session, spawn several ACP runs:
   ```
   sessions_spawn({ runtime: "acp", mode: "run", task: "..." })
   ```
2. Wait for all runs to complete (or not — the behavior is the same).
3. Reset the parent session via `/new` or `/reset`.
4. Check OS processes: all `claude-agent-acp` + `claude` pairs are still alive, reparented to systemd.

### Root cause analysis

**Two independent gaps** in the cleanup chain:

#### Gap 1: Oneshot close failure is silently swallowed

In the ACP session manager `runTurn()`, the oneshot cleanup runs in a `finally` block:

```js
if (meta.mode === "oneshot") {
  try {
    await runtime.close({ handle, reason: "oneshot-complete" });
  } catch (error) {
    logVerbose(`acp-manager: ACP oneshot close failed...`);
  }
}
```

If `runtime.close()` fails (e.g., stdin already closed, connection lost, timeout), the error is logged but **no PID-level fallback kill is attempted**. The process remains alive.

#### Gap 2: Parent session reset does not clean up child ACP sessions

`performGatewaySessionReset()` calls `cleanupSessionBeforeMutation()` which calls `closeAcpRuntimeForSession()` — but only for the **parent session key**. It does not:
- Query the store for child sessions spawned by this parent
- Iterate child ACP session keys to close their runtimes
- Kill processes associated with child ACP sessions

`stopSubagentsForRequester()` is called inside `ensureSessionRuntimeCleanup()`, but it only handles subagent-type sessions, not ACP sessions. The child ACP session entries have `spawnedBy` / `parentSessionKey` set, but these are never queried during parent cleanup.

### Suggested fix

###

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Hello, previously when I was using OpenClaw's session control browser, the command was displayed as SIGKILL. I installed OpenClaw in the WSL environment to control the host Chrome, and checked that the OpenClaw browser command was terminated by SIGKILL, causing the browser tool to timeout. I don't know if it's a bug (it can open tabs, but cannot open specific web pages)

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

OpenClaw 2026.4.15

Ubuntu connects to Windows 11

npm global

MiniMax-M2.7

minimax

_No response_

openclaw browser --browser-profile openclaw open https://baidu.com

命令又 SIGKILL 了。让我直接用工具打开：
浏览器工具超时了。gateway 可能需要重启。要我重启 gateway 吗？
进度报告：
- ✅ 成功重连 openclaw-tui（残留标签页已清除）
- ❌ openclaw browser 命令被 SIGKILL 终止
- ❌ 浏览器工具超时
- ⚠️ 浏览器驱动当前状态：running: false（已停止）
问题：在这个 WSL 镜像环境下，openclaw browser 命令似乎无法正常执行（总是被 SIGKILL）。浏览器驱动也因此停止了。
```

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

NOT_ENOUGH_INFO

target: openclaw/openclaw github_pr #69256: fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running
github_context:
GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 69256 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/69256 - Title: fix(cron): prevent premature session cleanup when subagents are running - State: OPEN - Author: lllyin - Labels: docs, size: S, triage: refactor-only, clawsweeper, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P1, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 session-state, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 4 - Changed files: CHANGELOG.md, docs/automation/cron-jobs.md, src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts, src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts Body: ```markdown ## Summary - **Problem**: Isolated cron sessions with `deleteAfterRun: true` are being deleted prematurely during a run if a "Direct Delivery" (structured content or threaded reply) is triggered, even if subagents (workers) are still active. - **Why it matters**: In orchestrator-worker patterns, the parent agent must survive to receive and aggregate results from its subagents. Premature cleanup orphans the workers and creates a fresh, context-less session, breaking the pipeline. - **What changed**: Modified `cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded` in `delivery-dispatch.ts` to check `countActiveDescendantRuns` before executing `sessions.delete`. - **What did NOT change**: The cleanup logic for standard text-only deliveries (which already had subagent awareness via `finalizeTextDelivery`) was not altered. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #67807 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) - **Root cause**: PR #67807 introduced automated session cleanup in the `finally` block of `deliverViaDirectAndCleanup` but missed the subagent-counting guardrail that exists in the sibling `finalizeTextDelivery` path. - **Missing detection / guardrail**: Lack of `countActiveDescendantRuns` verification in the Direct Delivery cleanup wrapper. - **Contributing context**: Complex workflows using Feishu or threaded replies trigger the `useDirectDelivery` path, bypassing the subagent waiting orchestration. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - Target test or file: `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: A cron job with `deleteAfterRun: true` that spawns a subagent and returns structured content should NOT trigger `sessions.delete` until the subagent completes. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It directly asserts that the gateway call is suppressed in the presence of active runs. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes None. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Bef [body truncated after 2500 chars] ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-20T07:35:15Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a subagent-count guard to `cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded` in `delivery-dispatch.ts`, mirroring the check that already existed in `finalizeTextDelivery`. When `countActiveDescendantRuns` returns a positive value, the `sessions.delete` call is skipped and the session is preserved for worker completion events, preventing orphaned workers in orchestrator-worker cron pipelines.

Confidence Score: 5/5

Safe to merge — the fix is minimal, well-targeted, and consistent with the sibling text-delivery path's existing pattern. The change is a one-for-one port of the guard already present in finalizeTextDelivery into cleanupDirectCronSessionIfNeeded. No new logic is introduced; the only new behaviour is a conditional early-return that keeps the session alive while workers are running. The session will still be deleted on a future invocation once countActiveDescendantRuns returns 0, so there is no session-leak risk. All remaining findings are P2 or lower. No files require special attention. Fixes #68656. Thanks @guti. - Feishu: recreate WebSocket clients with monitor-owned backoff only after SDK reconnect exhaustion, preserving heartbeat defaults and shutdown cleanup without treating recoverable SDK callback errors as terminal, so persistent connections recover without manual gateway restart. GitHub item: - Repository: openclaw/openclaw - Type: pull_request - Number: 69494 - URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/69494 - Title: fix: resolve SecretRef keychain tokens in desktop app browser proxy - State: OPEN - Author: shad0wca7 - Labels: app: macos, size: M, triage: needs-real-behavior-proof, P2, rating: 🧂 unranked krab, merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility, merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider, merge-risk: 🚨 availability, status: 📣 needs proof - Changed file count available to wrapper: 2 - Changed files: apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/GatewayEndpointStore.swift, apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/GatewayEndpointStoreTests.swift Body: ```markdown ## Problem When `gateway.auth.token` is configured as a SecretRef (e.g., `{source: "exec", provider: "keychain", id: "gateway-auth-token"}`), the macOS desktop app fails to resolve the actual token value. This causes: 1. Desktop app reads `gateway.auth.token` as a dictionary, not a string 2. The `as? String` cast fails → token resolves to nil 3. Browser proxy requests to the gateway control server have no auth header 4. Gateway returns 401 Unauthorized 5. All browser tool calls via `node.invoke` fail with `UNAVAILABLE: Unauthorized` ## Fix - Detect when `gateway.auth.token` or `gateway.auth.password` is a SecretRef dictionary with `provider: "keychain"` - Resolve the actual value from the macOS keychain using `/usr/bin/security find-generic-password` - Fall through to nil if resolution fails (preserving existing behavior) ## Testing - Verified the bug locally: `security find-generic-password -s gateway-auth-token -w` returns empty (wrong lookup), while `-s openclaw -a gateway-auth-token -w` returns the actual token - Confirmed browser control server accepts the token when passed via `Authorization: Bearer ` - The fix resolves the token correctly using the `service + account` keychain lookup pattern ``` Comments/context: ```markdown - greptile-apps at 2026-04-20T23:28:54Z:

Greptile Summary

This PR adds SecretRef keychain resolution for `gateway.auth.token` and `gateway.auth.password` in the macOS desktop app, spawning `/usr/bin/security find-generic-password` to retrieve the actual secret at runtime. - **P1 — SecretRef password resolution is dead code**: The new SecretRef handling in `resolveConfigPassword` is only reachable for the env-override warning check, not for actual password resolution. `resolveGatewayPassword` uses its own inline string cast for resolution and never delegates to `resolveConfigPassword`, so a `gateway.auth.password` configured as a SecretRef dict will still resolve to `nil`.

Confidence Score: 4/5

The token fix works correctly, but the password SecretRef path is unreachable, leaving that scenario broken despite the PR claiming to fix it. One P1 defect: the SecretRef handling added to resolveConfigPassword is never called during actual password resolution, so gateway.auth.password as a SecretRef still fails silently. The token fix (via resolveConfigToken) is correct. The blocking subprocess call is P2 only. apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/GatewayEndpointStore.swift — specifically resolveGatewayPassword, which needs to delegate to resolveConfigPassword for actual resolution, mirroring how resolveGatewayToken delegates to resolveConfigToken.

target: openclaw/openclaw github_issue #69943: [Bug]: session-memory hook persists raw chat-template tokens and unparsed tool calls — re-injected context creates self-reinforcing poisoning loop, agents emit role tokens / NO_REPLY across all subsequent /new sessions
github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: issue
- Number: 69943
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/69943
- Title: [Bug]: session-memory hook persists raw chat-template tokens and unparsed tool calls — re-injected context creates self-reinforcing poisoning loop, agents emit role tokens / NO_REPLY across all subsequent /new sessions
- State: OPEN
- Author: reidperyam
- Labels: bug

Body:
```markdown
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

The bundled session-memory hook saves raw model output to ~/.openclaw/workspace-<agent>/memory/<date>.md without sanitizing chat-template control tokens (`<|im_end|>`, `<|endoftext|>`), unmatched chat role markers (`assistant:`, `user:`), or unparsed `<tool_call>` XML emitted by quantized local models. The saved file is then re-injected as "Conversation Summary" context on the next /new or /reset, where the model interprets the embedded role markers as in-progress chat-template scaffolding and produces more malformed output (more orphaned role markers, NO_REPLY-only completions, or further raw tool-call XML). The hook then saves THAT malformed output as the next "memory" file, which gets re-injected on the following /new. Each /new degrades the agent further until it is functionally non-responsive. Disabling the hook globally (`openclaw hooks disable session-memory`) and quarantining the existing memory files is the only working workaround, since /new itself triggers re-injection.

This is distinct from #42112 (orphaned tool_use blocks rejected by the provider API) — there the corruption is in the session JSONL transcript and the provider returns 400. Here the corruption is in the hook-managed memory file, persists across /new (which #42112 says fixes that bug), and propagates across agents because every agent has its own session-memory file accumulating its own poison.

Observed across 5 of 6 active agent workspaces in our deployment, including both 9B (mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit on rapid-mlx) and 27B (mlx-community/Qwen3.5-27B-4bit-DWQ on rapid-mlx) brain agents. Both quantized local models periodically leak chat-template tokens during streaming; the hook captures and persists those leaks indiscriminately.

### Steps to reproduce

Preconditions: agent on a quantized local model server (we used mlx-community/Qwen3.5-9B-OptiQ-4bit served by rapid-mlx 0.3.12 on port 8001, but the saved-memory pattern below is provider-agnostic).

1. With session-memory hook enabled (default):

   openclaw hooks check
   # confirm: 💾 session-memory  enabled

2. Trigger any agent reply that emits a tool call. With Qwen3-family models on quantized backends, the SSE stream periodically leaks `<|im_end|>` and the raw `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` XML into the assistant content channel before the parser converts it. Auto-recovery handles tool execution correctly, but the leaked

[body truncated after 2500 chars]
```

### Root Cause Confirmed

`src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts` + `transcript.ts` — no sanitization anywhere in the write path.

**Write path (handler.ts:163-175):**
- `sessionContent` written verbatim — no sanitization pass

**Read path (transcript.ts:36-51):**
- `extractTextMessageContent` returns raw model output string
- Pushed directly to `allMessages` array → `sessionContent` — no stripping of any kind

**What happens:**
1. Model emits `<|im_end|>` / `<tool_call>...` in content block → `extractTextMessageContent` returns the raw string
2. Pushed directly to `allMessages` array → `sessionContent`
3. `handler.ts` writes `sessionContent` verbatim to memory file under "## Conversation Summary"
4. On next /new: hook reads memory file → injects as context → model sees chat-template scaffolding → emits more malformed output
5. Loop repeats → eventually model only emits NO_REPLY

**Bug is in two places:**
1. `transcript.ts:getRecentSessionContent()` — needs to strip chat-template tokens before pushing to `allMessages`
2. `handler.ts` — at minimum, the `sessionContent` should be sanitized before writing

**Fix needed:**
- Add sanitization regex in `transcript.ts` before return (strip `<|im_end|>`, `<|endoftext|>`, `<|im_start|>`, orphaned `^assistant:` / `^user:` at line starts, `<tool_call>.*?</tool_call>` XML blocks)
